content:
# 3 John Chapter 1 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/2JN_001_page_001.jpg

1. The elder to the beloved Gaius, whom I love in truth.

2. Beloved, concerning all things I pray you to prosper and to be in health, just as your soul prospers.

3. For I rejoiced greatly [when] brothers came and bore witness to your truth, just as you walk in truth.

4. I have no greater joy than these things, that I hear my children walking in the truth.

5. Beloved, you do faithfully whatever you work for the brothers, and this [for] strangers,

6. who bore witness to your love before [the] church. You will do well to send them on [their way] worthily of God,

7. for on behalf of the Name they went out, receiving nothing from the Gentiles.

8. We therefore ought to support such [ones], that we may become fellow workers for the truth.

9. I wrote something to the church, but Diotrephes, the [one] loving to be first among them, does not receive us.

10. Because of this, if I come, I will bring to remembrance his works which he does, speaking against us with evil words. And not being satisfied with these, he himself does not receive the brothers, and those wanting [to do so] he forbids and casts [them] out of the church.

11. Beloved, do not imitate the evil but the good. The [one] doing good is of God; the [one] doing evil has not seen God.

12. Demetrius has been borne witness to by all and by the truth itself. And we also bear witness, and you know that our witness is true.

13. I had many things to write to you, but I do not wish to write to you with ink and pen.

14. But I hope to see you shortly, and we will speak mouth to mouth.

15. Peace to you. The friends greet you. Greet the friends by name.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.7 — "On behalf of the Name":** hyper tou onomatos — "the Name" without specifying whose. In early Christian usage, "the Name" means the name of Jesus Christ. The capitalization reflects its use as a quasi-title.

2. **v.9 — "Diotrephes, the one loving to be first":** ho philoprōteuōn — a hapax legomenon (used only here in the NT). The word literally means "the one who loves to be first/preeminent."

3. **v.15 — "The friends":** hoi philoi — the Johannine community's self-designation as "friends" (cf. John 15:15, "I have called you friends").

— EMET, AI Translator
